title: Dyckia yunguensis
taxonomy: Dyckia yunguensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is native to Bolivia and is a type of succulent, characterized by its thick, waxy leaves. Dyckia yunguensis is a member of the genus Dyckia, which is a group of plants commonly known as "pin-cushion bromeliads" due to their dense, spiny rosettes of leaves. This genus is further classified within the subfamily Tillandsioideae, which is a large and diverse group of bromeliads. Dyckia yunguensis is an example of a plant that has adapted to thrive in dry, rocky environments.
